rulings.Case Title: ROBERT CULVER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 8, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Honeywell International Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. sections 437c(a) and 1010.6, CRC 2.251 and G.O. 158, which applies pursuant to the asbestos Case Management Order. Defendant¿s proof of service shows that defendant electronically served its motion on August 24, 2012. G.O. 158 explicitly states that the filing and service provisions of C.C.P. section 1010.6 shall apply. With a noticed hearing date of November 8, 2012, defendant failed to provide proper notice by failing to extend the notice period by two court days as required by C.C.P. 1010.6. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE MORRIS et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIAL COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 8, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 17, Defendant University Mechanical & Engineering Contractors, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication (strict liability, premises owner/contractor liability, false representation and intentional tort causes of action) are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence (1) that Ms. Morris was exposed to asbestos-containing products or material attributable to defendant and that such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to her asbestos-related illness, (2) that defendant breached a duty owed to Ms. Morris, or (3) that all of plaintiff¿s claims against defendant are barred by Workers Compensation exclusivity. By failing to submit with its moving papers verified factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his claims against defendant, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff¿s objections on the grounds of defendant¿s failure to submit verifications are sustained. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) Defendant also failed to submit evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiff¿s claims against defendant. As to summary adjudication, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his causes of action for strict liability, premises owner/contractor liability, false representation and intentional tort. By failing to submit with its moving papers verified factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support these claims, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff¿s objections on the grounds of defendant¿s failure to submit verifications are sustained. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) Defendant also failed to submit evidence to negate an essential element of any of these causes of action. Plaintiff¿s objections to Mr. Novak¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)
